plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to eliminate risk.
Trim QQQI and CRWD to reduce concentration.
Reduce SPY to meet single-name limits.
Buy NVDA as a top-rated long setup.
Hold significant cash for safety.
Focus on capital preservation and low drawdown.
conservative.read: Targeting ~1.5% monthly return with a focus on capital preservation. This plan significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, holding a larger cash buffer for stability. Expect lower potential gains but also a much lower chance of significant drawdowns.

plan: Trim SOXL and sell SPAL to reduce leverage.
Trim QQQI and CRWD to lower sector concentration.
Reduce SPY to meet single-name limits.
Add NVDA and MSFT as strong long setups.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer.
Balance risk reduction with growth opportunities.
balanced.read: Targeting ~2.5% monthly return with a risk-first growth approach. This plan reduces leverage and concentration while adding quality long setups. It aims for steady growth with controlled drawdowns, maintaining a balance between risk and reward.

plan: Increase SOXL exposure for aggressive growth.
Trim QQQI to free up capital.
Reduce SPY to manage concentration.
Add to CRWD and buy NVDA for high conviction.
Add GUSH as a high potential R-value setup.
Maintain a small cash buffer, accepting higher risk.
aggressive.read: Targeting ~4% monthly return through aggressive growth and leverage. This plan concentrates on high-scoring long setups and increases leveraged ETF exposure. Expect significantly higher potential gains but also substantially larger drawdowns and amplified volatility due to leverage and daily decay.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and volatile tech.
Maximize QQQI for steady income generation.
Trim SPY to reduce growth focus.
Add OXY for energy sector income.
Hold a significant cash buffer for stability.
Prioritize reliable monthly distributions over growth.
income.read: Targeting ~1% monthly return with a focus on stable income distributions. This plan eliminates leverage and high-beta growth names, shifting heavily into income-generating assets like QQQI. Expect lower NAV volatility and consistent payouts, though distributions are not guaranteed and NAV can still decline.
